title: Command-palette-first
summary: WordPress Playground reimagined as a single fuzzy-search palette: every panel, blueprint, file, setting, log line is a command.

title: IDE-like
summary: WordPress Playground reimagined as a VS Code-style IDE: activity bar, tabbed workspace, status bar, command palette.

title: Notion-like
summary: Playground as a workspace: nested page tree on the left, every action lives inside the page itself, no floating toolbar.

title: Mac Finder windows
summary: Playground as a macOS desktop: every site is a draggable window with traffic-lights, sites/blueprints/files browsed as Finder folders.

title: Browser-tab metaphor
summary: Playground becomes a browser: real tab strip per site, URL bar for WP paths, bookmarks bar of blueprints, DevTools drawer for files/DB/logs.

title: Mobile-first
summary: A thumb-first Playground: phone-sized chrome with a bottom tab bar and slide-up sheets; desktop is just a wider phone with side rails.

title: Dark cyberpunk hacker terminal
summary: Green-on-black CRT bezel running WordPress like a wp-cli session — every action a copyable command.

title: Apple Vision / spatial
summary: Floating glass panels in 3D space — the WordPress iframe is a window, tools hover beside it as parallaxed cards.

title: Storybook-style component library
summary: Playgrounds become components, snapshots become stories, the iframe is the canvas, and PHP/WP settings live in a Storybook controls panel.

title: Figma-like
summary: An infinite zoomable canvas where every playground is a draggable frame; left layers, right inspector, top toolbar.

title: Newspaper / magazine layout
summary: The Playground Press: serif-broadsheet edition of WordPress Playground with the live site as today's lede and blueprints as columns.

title: Game launcher (Steam)
summary: WordPress Playground reimagined as Steam: a library of blueprints, a giant hero with a green PLAY button, and 'Now Playing' state.

title: Game launcher (Epic-style)
summary: WordPress Playground as the Epic Games Store: a cinematic rotating hero, animated tile rails, and a vertical icon nav.

title: macOS Settings.app (Ventura style)
summary: WordPress Playground reimagined as macOS Ventura's System Settings — a categorized sidebar with a Live Preview pane, blueprint browser, file manager and logs as rows.

title: Windows 11 Settings
summary: Playground as the Windows 11 Settings.app: Mica backdrops, pill-shaped categories and filters, breadcrumbs, and lots of whitespace.

title: GitHub repo page
summary: WordPress Playground reimagined as a GitHub repo: Code is the iframe, Issues are logs, PRs boot from WP/Gutenberg, Actions runs blueprints.

title: Linear / Vercel / minimal
summary: A monochrome, keyboard-first chrome for Playground: hairline borders, JetBrains Mono labels, ⌘K everywhere, accent reserved for one action.

title: Discord layout
summary: Playgrounds as servers, Files/Blueprint/Logs as channels, the iframe as a pinned message, logs as a chat-like stream.

title: Slack layout
summary: Saved playgrounds are workspaces, Site-Manager tabs are channels, and PHP logs live as a threaded conversation in the right pane.

title: Email client (Mail.app)
summary: A three-column Mail.app metaphor: blueprint categories as mailboxes, saved playgrounds as messages, the live WordPress site as the reading pane.

title: Spotify-like
summary: Playground as a music player: library of saved sites, shelves of blueprint albums, the iframe as the now-playing record, transport bar at the bottom.

title: Music DAW / Ableton
summary: WordPress Playground as a digital audio workstation: blueprint steps are track clips on a timeline, the iframe is the master output, and you compose sites like songs.

title: Kanban
summary: Blueprints as cards across category columns; drag onto the Launch lane to boot a playground.

title: Tile dashboard (Windows 8 / live tiles)
summary: A Metro-inspired Start screen of resizable, color-blocked live tiles where every saved site, blueprint, and tool flips through its own status.

title: Card stack (Tinder-style)
summary: Swipe through 43 blueprints like dating cards — right to launch, left to skip, up for details, down to save.

title: Wizard / step-by-step
summary: A friendly, hand-holding wizard that turns every Playground action into a clear numbered step with a progress bar.

title: Single-page scroll
summary: A scrollable document where every Playground surface lives in one column beside a sticky iframe — no modals, no panels, just sections.

title: Two-up split view
summary: WordPress Playground reimagined as a video editor: the iframe is the program monitor up top, and every control lives on a resizable timeline/inspector bay below.

title: Console + visual hybrid
summary: Polished visual chrome on top, a real bottom-docked terminal below — every click echoes as a wp-cli command you can copy.

title: Excel / spreadsheet
summary: WordPress Playground as a spreadsheet: blueprints and saved sites are rows, properties are columns, the iframe is a preview cell.

title: Trello / sticky notes
summary: A literal corkboard where every saved playground, blueprint, and tool is a draggable sticky note you pin, peel and pop open.

title: Concept-album minimal
summary: Just the iframe and a single dot. Tap it; a radial pie unfolds every Playground action like a spinning record sleeve.

title: Radial / pie menu
summary: Every Playground action is a petal on a hover-disclosed pie. A fixed central hub orbits the iframe; rings expand outward into blueprints, files, logs.

title: Sketch / Excalidraw hand-drawn
summary: WordPress Playground as a designer's sketchbook: hand-drawn fonts, dashed borders, sticky-note saves, and highlighter-tape featured blueprints.

title: Brutalist web 1.0
summary: A monospaced, 1px-bordered newspaper grid that treats Playground like a public-records terminal from 1997.

title: Y2K / Aero Glass
summary: WordPress Playground reimagined as a glossy Aero-era OS: chromatic-aberration chrome, beveled jellybean buttons, and frosted glass panels.

title: Memphis / 80s
summary: WordPress Playground as a Memphis-Milano poster: hot pink, teal, squiggles, geometric confetti, and chunky black borders around every panel.

title: Glassmorphism + heavy blur
summary: The WordPress iframe fills the canvas; all chrome floats above as frosted, blurred glass that lets the site's color shine through.

title: Neumorphism
summary: A pillowy monochrome workspace where every control is softly embossed or pressed into a single warm clay surface.

title: High-contrast accessibility-first
summary: WCAG AAA contrast, 56px hit targets, persistent keyboard hints, and prominent focus rings make every Playground feature obvious and operable.

title: Print-document inspired
summary: WordPress Playground reimagined as a typeset A4 manuscript: serif body, marginalia tips, footnotes, and a card-catalog blueprint index.

title: Comic book
summary: WordPress Playground reimagined as a comic strip: bold ink outlines, halftone shadows, speech-bubble tooltips, and KAPOW! action panels.

title: Childrens-book / kid-friendly
summary: A round, crayon-warm storybook UI where Wapuu the mascot guides you through every screen of WordPress Playground.

title: Wapuu-centric mascot UI
summary: Wapuu lives in a left rail, narrating state changes and quietly recommending blueprints — a polished pro UI with a friendly heartbeat.

title: WordPress Studio / Local-by-Flywheel-like
summary: A native desktop app feel: each playground is an environment with a Running/Stopped state, big Open Site buttons, and tabbed inspectors.

title: Vercel/Netlify dashboard
summary: Each playground is a deployment. Save = Promote to Production. Build logs slide up like a real DX dashboard.

title: Stripe Dashboard
summary: Playground reimagined as a financial control room: hero metric, time-series of boots, ledger-style playgrounds, blueprint catalog.

title: Apple Music Now Playing
summary: WordPress Playground reimagined as Apple Music: blueprints are songs, the iframe is album art, Your Playgrounds is your library.

title: Maps
summary: A pannable cartographic atlas where WordPress versions, PHP versions, and blueprints become regions, harbors and pinned destinations you sail to.

title: Tube / metro line map
summary: WordPress and PHP versions are stations on parallel transit lines; blueprints are interchanges. Pick a station, board a Playground.

title: Timeline / Git log
summary: Playground as a version-controlled graph: every Save is a commit, every fork a branch, Reset is checkout main.

title: Branching tree (org-chart)
summary: Your playground history is a horizontal fork-able org-chart; every node is a snapshot you can branch, inspect, or boot.

title: Tabs-only
summary: No sidebars, no drawers. Every panel — Site, Settings, Files, Blueprint, DB, Logs, Launcher — is a tab on one strip.

title: Floating action button (Material You)
summary: A radial Material You FAB orbits every Playground action while the iframe enjoys an edge-to-edge stage with dynamic color.

title: iPad multitasking
summary: WordPress on the left, a swappable Split View pane on the right, with an iPadOS pull-tab divider, Slide Over launcher, and a Dock of saved sites.

title: Stage Manager
summary: Each playground is a macOS-style Stage with floating windows; the left rail holds your other Spaces, the launcher is a stage too.

title: Three-pane Outlook/Mail
summary: Playground reimagined as Outlook: folder nav, an Inbox of things-you-can-do, and a reading pane that is your live WordPress.

title: Single dropdown
summary: All chrome collapsed behind one brutalist menu top-right. Just the iframe and a button. Helvetica, hard edges, no decoration.

title: Fullscreen iframe + heads-up overlay
summary: Chrome hides like a video player: the WordPress iframe fills the viewport; toolbars and panels glide in from the edges on hover.

title: Video player chrome
summary: WordPress Playground as a media player: timeline scrubber of state checkpoints, transport controls, playlist of blueprints.

title: Time-machine
summary: Every change creates a frame in a 3D receding timeline you can scrub, fork, or restore — Playground as a time machine.

title: Hub-and-spoke
summary: Mission-control hub: the running site sits at the center of a radial console; major actions are orbital spokes that flip open into focused sub-screens.

title: Reader-first
summary: The WordPress canvas gets 75% of the screen; everything else hides in a thin icon rail with tooltips that bloom into panels on demand.

title: Inspector-first (devtools)
summary: WordPress Playground as a Chrome DevTools session: iframe up top, dockable inspector below with Logs, Blueprint, Files, DB tabs.

title: Twitter-like timeline
summary: Playground as a social feed: each saved site and blueprint is a post, the iframe is the thread detail, logs are replies.

title: TweetDeck multi-column
summary: Playground as a curated deck of narrow vertical columns - one per playground, blueprint category, logs feed, or panel.

title: Pinterest grid
summary: A masonry board of blueprint pins with rich previews — discovery first, the live WordPress iframe pinned as the active board.

title: Apple TV grid
summary: A cinematic dark home screen of horizontal poster shelves — featured, recents, theme- and plugin-author picks — that boots a WordPress in one click.

title: Channel guide / TV grid
summary: WordPress Playground as cable TV: the iframe is a screen, blueprints are channels, and the EPG is a 2D grid you 'tune in' to.

title: Onboarding-driven
summary: A first-run persona pick (Theme / Plugin / Site owner / Learner) bends the chrome, defaults, and shelves — never hides features.

title: Persona-tabbed top nav
summary: Four persona tabs at the very top retune the entire chrome — curated blueprints, default Site Manager tab, and tool emphasis follow your role.

title: Pricing-page-style for blueprints
summary: Choose your starter: blueprints styled as a SaaS pricing page with tier columns, feature checkmarks, and a Compare-all table.

title: Restaurant-menu style
summary: WordPress Playground as a bistro: blueprints arranged as a printed menu with a sommelier helper that pairs sites to your taste.

title: Cookbook
summary: WordPress Playground reimagined as a cookbook: blueprints are recipes with ingredients, prep time, and a step-by-step method.

title: Catalog / IKEA
summary: WordPress Playground reimagined as an IKEA catalog: every blueprint is a numbered SKU with a product photo, spec tag, and assembly steps.

title: Drag-and-drop blueprint builder
summary: A node-based visual canvas where blueprint steps are draggable cards wired into a flowchart. Run the flow = boot the site.

title: Block-editor styled (Gutenberg-inside-Gutenberg)
summary: The Playground's chrome IS the block editor: the iframe is a 'site block' with toolbar, inserter and inspector around it.

title: wp-admin styled (faux-WP)
summary: Playground chrome rebuilt as wp-admin itself: admin bar + left menu wrap the iframe so the tooling feels native to WordPress.

title: Dual-iframe split
summary: Two synchronized Playgrounds side-by-side for A/B comparison of WP versions, PHP runtimes, blueprints, and PRs.

title: Multi-pane workspace
summary: Playground as a 4-quadrant workspace: preview, blueprint, files, logs — each quadrant maximizable, swappable, scriptable.

title: Floating widgets desktop
summary: A Playground OS where every feature is a draggable, resizable widget on a wallpapered desktop, with layouts saved per playground.

title: Single-line top toolbar, everything else collapses
summary: One slim toolbar, four edge handles. Site Manager, Launcher, Settings and Logs each slide in from their own screen edge.

title: Bottom sheet only
summary: Every Playground control lives in a swipe-up sheet over a full-bleed WordPress wallpaper — mobile-app ergonomics, even at 1440 wide.

title: Sidebar that swaps content
summary: One sidebar, six personalities — an activity column reshapes the panel into Files, Blueprint, DB, Logs, Settings, or Site mode.

title: Two-tier vertical nav
summary: A thin icon rail picks the workspace; a wider rail lists its items; the iframe fills the rest — every surface, two clicks away.

title: Top + bottom toolbar (no sidebars)
summary: Twin rails sandwich the iframe: top handles identity and navigation, bottom is a context-swapping tool dock that morphs per mode.

title: Pull-to-refresh + edge gestures
summary: Every panel lives off-screen behind a labeled edge handle; mobile-app gestures (pull, swipe, drag) become the primary, discoverable controls.

title: Voice/keyboard-prompt-driven
summary: A single Quick-prompt input replaces every toolbar; type or speak verbs like 'switch to PHP 7.4' or 'show logs' to drive Playground.

title: AR/HUD military aesthetic
summary: Tactical heads-up display with targeting reticles, monospaced telemetry, and persistent status corners surrounding the iframe.

title: Sci-fi terminal (Severance / Alien)
summary: MU/TH/UR 6000 meets Lumon MDR: vector-line CRT chrome around WordPress, eerie monospace telemetry, every action a soft beep.

title: Soft-pastel cozy
summary: A warm, marshmallow-soft WordPress Playground in pastel peach, lavender and mint — rounded everything, friendly micro-copy, zero hostility.
